        streamToFile
      • streamToFile(req: HttpRequest; tmpDir = ""): string

High-performance HTTP/1.1 request parser.

Design goals:

  • Incremental: feed bytes as they arrive from the socket
  • Zero-copy: parse into byte offsets, materialize strings lazily
  • Minimal allocations: only allocate when data is accessed
  • Fast method dispatch: switch on first byte for O(1) method detection

Uses std/httpcore types: HttpMethod, HttpCode, HttpHeaders, HttpVersion.

Imports

simdscan, ../net/tcp, ../loop

Types

BodyStream = object
A stream for reading the request body in chunks. 
HttpBodyCallback = proc (data: openArray[byte]; done: bool) {.closure.}
Callback invoked as body data arrives from the network. When set on an HttpParser, body bytes are streamed to this callback instead of being buffered in the parser. The callback receives raw (non-chunk-decoded) data for Content-Length bodies, or decoded data for chunked transfer encoding. done is true when this is the last body chunk. 
HttpParser = ref object
  maxBodySize*: int64        ## Max body size (0 = unlimited)
  connectionClose*: bool     ## Connection: close seen
  onBodyData*: HttpBodyCallback ## Called as body bytes arrive (nil = buffered mode)
  bodyStreamed*: int64       ## Bytes streamed via callback so far
  streamingBody*: bool       ## true when body is being streamed to callback
  methodCache*: HttpMethod
Incremental HTTP/1.1 request parser. 
HttpRequest = ref object
  parser*: HttpParser
  httpMethod*: HttpMethod
  streamer*: MultipartStreamerRef
  streamPath*: string
  urlVal*: string
  headersVal*: HttpHeaders
  headersReady*: bool
  bodyVal*: seq[byte]
  bodyReady*: bool
A parsed HTTP request with lazy accessor methods. 
ParsePhase = enum
  PhaseRequestLine,         ## Parsing the request line
  PhaseHeaders,             ## Parsing header lines
  PhaseBody,                ## Reading body (if Content-Length > 0)
  PhaseComplete,            ## Request fully parsed
  PhaseError                 ## Parse error occurred
Parser state machine phases.

Consts

DefaultBodyBuf = 65536
Default body read buffer 
MaxHeaders = 100
Max number of headers 
MaxHeaderSize = 8192
Max header section size in bytes (default 8KB) 
MaxRequestLine = 8192
Max request line size

Procs

proc bodyView(req: HttpRequest): tuple[data: ptr UncheckedArray[byte], len: int] {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Zero-copy pointer into the parser buffer for the entire body. No allocation. The pointer is valid for the duration of the request handler. 
proc drainChunk(stream: var BodyStream; len: Natural) {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Advances the read position by len bytes after processing a peeked chunk. 
proc error(p: HttpParser): HttpCode {.inline, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc feed(p: HttpParser; data: openArray[byte]): ParsePhase {.discardable,
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}

Feed raw bytes from the network into the parser. Returns the current parse phase after processing.

Keep calling feed() as data arrives. When the return value is PhaseComplete, the request is ready. PhaseError means bad request.

If onBodyData is set, body bytes are streamed to the callback instead of being buffered in p.buf. This dramatically reduces memory for large uploads since p.buf only needs to hold headers.

proc feed(p: HttpParser; data: string): ParsePhase {.inline, discardable,
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Convenience overload for feeding string data. 
proc getBody(req: HttpRequest): seq[byte] {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Get the request body. Returns empty seq if no body. 
proc getBodyStream(req: HttpRequest): BodyStream {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Returns a BodyStream for reading the request body in chunks. 
proc getBodyString(req: HttpRequest): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Get the request body as a string. 
proc getBodyView(p: HttpParser): tuple[data: ptr UncheckedArray[byte], len: int] {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Zero-copy pointer into the parser buffer for the entire body. No allocation. The pointer is valid until feed() is called again.

For Content-Length bodies: points to p.bufp.headerEnd, len = contentLength. For chunked bodies: points to decoded data in p.bufp.headerEnd, len = chunkBodyLen. Returns (nil, 0) if no body is present.

proc getConnectionClose(req: HttpRequest): bool {.inline, ...raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Returns true if the client sent "Connection: close". 
proc getContentLength(req: HttpRequest): int {.inline, ...raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Get the Content-Length value, or -1 if not present. 
proc getHeaders(req: HttpRequest): HttpHeaders {....raises: [KeyError], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Parse and return headers. Materialized on first call, then cached. 
proc getMethod(req: HttpRequest): HttpMethod {.inline, ...raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Get the HTTP method. Zero-alloc — already resolved during parse. 
proc getMultipart(req: HttpRequest; tmpDir = ""): MultipartStreamerRef {....raises: [
    KeyError, OSError, IOError, Exception, MultipartSizeLimitError, ValueError], tags: [
    ReadEnvEffect, ReadIOEffect, WriteDirEffect, ReadDirEffect, RootEffect,
    TimeEffect, WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}

Lazily parse multipart/form-data from the request body on first call. Returns a MultipartStreamerRef with parsed boundaries, or nil if the Content-Type is not multipart/form-data.

Uses bodyView() internally for zero-copy access to the parser buffer. Feeds in 64KB chunks for lightweight per-chunk processing (magic number checking, file writes, etc). For streaming routes (auto-detected multipart Content-Type), the parser buffer is bypassed entirely and req.streamer is pre-populated — returns immediately with zero additional work.

Memory (lazy, already-buffered body): parser buffer + 64KB write buffer. Memory (streaming, auto-detected): ~4KB headers + 64KB write buffer.

Usage: let mp = req.getMultipart() if mp != nil and mp.isComplete(): for b in mp.boundaries(): ... mp.cleanup()

proc getPath(req: HttpRequest): lent string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc getQuery(req: HttpRequest): lent string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc getRemainingData(p: HttpParser): seq[byte] {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Return any unconsumed bytes after the HTTP headers (and body, if present). Useful after an HTTP/1.1 upgrade (e.g. WebSocket) where extra bytes from the initial TCP read may contain the first protocol frames. 
proc getRequest(p: HttpParser): HttpRequest {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Create a request view from the parser. Only valid when isComplete(). 
proc getUrl(req: HttpRequest): lent string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc headerBytes(req: HttpRequest): int {.inline, ...raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Total bytes consumed by the request line + headers + rnrn. 
proc isComplete(p: HttpParser): bool {.inline, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc isError(p: HttpParser): bool {.inline, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc newHttpParser(initialBufSize = 4096): HttpParser {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Create a new HTTP request parser. 
proc peekAll(stream: BodyStream): tuple[data: ptr UncheckedArray[byte], len: int] {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Zero-copy view of the entire remaining body. No allocation. The pointer is valid for the duration of the request handler. 
proc peekChunk(stream: var BodyStream; maxLen: Natural): tuple[
    data: ptr UncheckedArray[byte], len: int] {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Returns a pointer and length to the next available chunk (up to maxLen). No copy is performed. The pointer is only valid until the next buffer operation. 
proc peekContentType(p: HttpParser): lent string {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
proc peekMethod(p: HttpParser): HttpMethod {.inline, ...raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Get the HTTP method. Zero-alloc — already resolved during parse. 
proc peekPath(p: HttpParser): lent string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
proc phase(p: HttpParser): ParsePhase {.inline, ...raises: [], tags: [],
                                        forbids: [].}
proc readChunk(stream: var BodyStream; maxLen: Natural): seq[byte] {....raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Reads up to maxLen bytes from the body stream. Returns empty seq when no more data is available. 
proc readChunkInto(stream: var BodyStream; buf: var seq[byte]; maxLen: Natural): int {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Reads up to maxLen bytes into a pre-allocated buffer. Returns the number of bytes written (0 = EOF). The caller can reuse buf across calls — no per-chunk allocation. 
proc readChunkString(stream: var BodyStream; maxLen: Natural): string {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Reads up to maxLen bytes from the body stream as a string. 
proc reset(p: HttpParser) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Reset the parser for a new request (keep-alive reuse). 
proc resetForNext(p: HttpParser) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Reset the parser for the next pipelined request, preserving any unconsumed bytes in the buffer (e.g. bytes belonging to a subsequent request that arrived in the same TCP read).

For streaming mode, the buffer has already been rearranged to contain only leftover bytes from the next request — no copy needed.

proc streamToFile(req: HttpRequest; tmpDir = ""): string {.
    ...raises: [OSError, IOError], tags: [ReadEnvEffect, ReadIOEffect,
                                       WriteDirEffect, ReadDirEffect,
                                       TimeEffect, WriteIOEffect], forbids: [].}

Stream the request body (or re-stream from buffer) to a temp file. Returns the temp file path. The caller should delete the file when done.

If the route uses streamToFile = true, the body was already streamed and this just returns req.streamPath. Otherwise, it feeds body bytes from the parser buffer to a temp file in 64KB chunks — zero extra copies beyond the one already in the parser buffer.

Usage: let path = req.streamToFile() defer: removeFile(path) # process path...


Made with Nim. Generated: 2026-06-18 01:52:04 UTC
